name: Carrot-Raisin Muffins
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Raisins, soaked in hot water
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Spray muffin tins with nonstick vegetable spray (spraying paper muffin cups is recommended for low-fat muffins that tend to stick).
3. In a large bowl, combine dry ingredients: flour, wheat germ, baking powder, baking soda, salt, brown sugar substitute, cinnamon, cloves, and nutmeg.
4. Stir in shredded carrots and drained raisins.
5. In a separate bowl, beat eggs with oil until well combined.
6. Stir the egg mixture into the flour mixture until just combined.
7. Divide the batter evenly among 12 muffin cups.
8. Bake for 30-35 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center of a muffin comes out clean.
Nutritional Information:
1 muffin - 105 calories, 1 starch gram, 1/2 fat exchange (4 grams fat, 16 grams carbohydrates, 1 gram fiber, 213 mg sodium, 118 mg potassium, 46 mg cholesterol)
Source: American Diabetes Association Family Cookbook Volume III, 1987. Shared but not tested by Elizabeth Rodier, December 1993.
